channel.title: LayoutLMv2 on Kimberly's Blog
channel.description: Recent content in LayoutLMv2 on Kimberly's Blog

item.title: LayoutLMv2 Review
item.description:
In this post, we will review Microsoft’s 2021 paper: “LayoutLMv2: Multi-modal Pre-training for Visually-rich Document Understanding.”
https://arxiv.org/pdf/2012.14740.pdf

Table of Contents

  1. Model Architecture
  2. Pre-training Tasks
  3. Training

Model Architecture

Text Embedding

  • Uses text sequences extracted via OCR.
  • Utilizes the WordPiece tokenizer.
  • Prepends [CLS] to the sequence and [SEP] to the end of each text segment.
  • Pads with [PAD] tokens until the maximum sequence length $L$ is reached.
  • Applies 1D positional embeddings.
  • Assigns each token a segment $ s_i \in \{[A], [B]\} $.
  • The final $i$-th $(0 \leq i < L)$ text embedding is:
    • $ t_i = TokEmb(w_i) + PosEmb1D(i) + SegEmb(s_i) $

Visual Embedding

  • Resizes the input image to 224×224 pixels.
  • Uses ResNeXt-FPN as the visual backbone to extract feature maps; its parameters are updated during backpropagation.
  • The feature map is average-pooled to width $W$ and height $H$ (paper uses $W = H = 7$).
  • Flattens this to a $W \times H$-length vector $ VisTokEmb(I) $.
  • Applies a linear projection layer to match the size of the text embeddings.
  • Since CNNs do not inherently encode position, the same 1D positional embeddings as text tokens are added.
  • The segment used for visual tokens is [C].
  • The $i$-th $(0 \leq i < WH)$ visual embedding is:
    • $ v_i = Proj(VisTokEmb(I)_i) + PosEmb1D(i) + SegEmb([C]) $

Layout Embedding

  • Embeds the bounding box information that comes from OCR results.
  • Normalizes the box coordinates to integers in [0, 1000].
  • For the $i$-th $(0 \leq i < WH + L)$ text/visual token with $box_i = (x_{min}, x_{max}, y_{min}, y_{max}, width, height)$, the layout embedding is:
    • $ l_i = Concat(PosEmb2D_x(x_{min}, x_{max}, width), PosEmb2D_y(y_{min}, y_{max}, height)) $
  • For visual tokens, the bounding box corresponds to the position in the $W \times H$ grid. For $W = H = 7$, $box_0 = (0, 0, 142, 142, 142, 142)$ where $142 = int(1000/7)$.
  • Special tokens [CLS], [SEP], [PAD] use $ box_{PAD} = (0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0) $.

Multi-modal Encoder

  • Input
    • Concatenates the visual embeddings and text embeddings into a single sequence:
      • $ X = \{\textbf{v}_0, ..., \textbf{v}_{WH-1}, \textbf{t}_0, ..., \textbf{t}_{L-1}\} $
    • Adds the corresponding layout embedding to the above sequence for the final input:
      • For $0 \leq i < WH + L$, $ \textbf{x}_i^{(0)} = X_i + \textbf{l}_i $
  • Spatial-Aware Self-Attention
    • Let hidden size be $d_{head}$ and projections $\textbf{W}^Q, \textbf{W}^K, \textbf{W}^V$. The self-attention score is:
      • $ \alpha_{ij} = \frac{1}{\sqrt{d_{head}}} (\textbf{x}_i \textbf{W}^Q) (\textbf{x}_j \textbf{W}^K)^\top $
    • $ \textbf{b}^{(1D)} $ encodes relative distance in the token sequence.
    • $ \textbf{b}^{(2D_x)}, \textbf{b}^{(2D_y)} $ encode the spatial distance between token bounding boxes in x and y directions.
    • The spatial-aware attention score is:
      • $ \alpha_{ij}^{\prime} = \alpha_{ij} + \textbf{b}^{(1D)} + \textbf{b}^{(2D_x)} + \textbf{b}^{(2D_y)} $
    • The output vector $h_i$ is:
      • $ \textbf{h}_i = \displaystyle{\sum_{j}} \frac{\exp(\alpha_{ij}^{\prime})}{\sum_{k} \exp(\alpha_{ik}^{\prime})} \textbf{x}_j \textbf{W}^V $


Source: Xu, Yang, et al. “Layoutlmv2: Multi-modal pre-training for visually-rich document understanding.” arXiv preprint arXiv:2012.14740 (2020), Figure 1.

Pre-training Tasks

Masked Visual-Language Modeling (MVLM)

  • Teaches the model to better understand language using cross-modality information.
  • Randomly masks text tokens and requires the model to predict them.
  • In this paper, 15% of text tokens are masked; 80% are replaced with [MASK], 10% with a random token from the vocabulary, and 10% remain unchanged.
  • The corresponding regions in the image are masked as well.
  • Layout information is left unmasked.
  • The final cross-entropy loss is computed by feeding the last output of the masked token to a classifier.

Text-Image Alignment (TIA)

  • Randomly selects a token line and covers its region in the image. Lines are covered at the line level rather than the word level.
  • 15% of lines are covered in the paper.
  • The model predicts whether the output of a token corresponds to [Covered] or [Not Covered] using a classifier attached to the output of the covered tokens.
  • Tokens masked for MVLM are excluded from TIA loss calculation.

Text-Image Matching (TIM)

  • Classifier attached to the [CLS] output to predict whether the image and text come from the same document.
  • The original image is used as a positive sample.
  • Negative samples are produced by swapping with images from other documents or omitting the image (missing image).
  • 15% of images are swapped and 5% are omitted in experiments.
  • Negative samples are also masked and covered; in negative TIM, all TIA targets are [Covered].
  • Binary cross-entropy loss is used.

Training

Data


Source: Xu, Yang, et al. “Layoutlmv2: Multi-modal pre-training for visually-rich document understanding.” arXiv preprint arXiv:2012.14740 (2020), Table 1.

Settings

  • The text encoder uses UniLMv2.
  • The visual embedding uses ResNeXt-FPN, the backbone from Mask-RCNN.
  • Other parameters are initialized randomly.
  • All parameters are trained end-to-end.

Pre-training

  • Utilizes samples from the IIT-CDIP dataset.
  • Sets maximum sequence length $L=512$, all text tokens are assigned segment [A].
  • The vision encoder’s output shape is set to $W=H=7$, producing a feature map of 49 tokens.

Fine-tuning

  • For document-level classification, the [CLS] token’s output is used.
  • Entity extraction and DocVQA tasks add a task-specific head to the model.
